author: jwasinger
content:
geth was changed to not include the BAL size in the cap, but before that change, it was failing some of the 7934 tests: https://gist.github.com/jwasinger/6a988169114e05545322d9b51f448ec2

The purpose of 7934 is to cap the block size to ensure that we can propagate blocks at the head.   imo it makes sense to include the BAL size in the cap, even if it won't be hit at current gas limits.

Also I think it would simplify the static validation of BALs.

author: nero_eth
content:
I don’t think the rationale behind 7934 was to ensure blocks are proposed and propagated faster at the tip of the chain. Blocks that approach the 8 MiB limit are typically highly compressible (mostly zeros) and end up significantly smaller than blocks containing ~70% random non-zero bytes, which compress much worse and can lead to larger Snappy-compressed blocks.
My understanding is that 7934 is more of a safety measure. Since we can’t cap compressed block size directly, the cap was applied to the uncompressed size instead.
That said, I agree the BAL should count toward this cap as well. This would just require updating the tests.
